args.name: petTypeId
args.description: The numeric ID of the pet type to retrieve. Example: 1

args.name: petId
args.description: The numeric ID of the pet to retrieve. Example: 3

args.name: ownerId
args.description: The numeric ID of the owner who owns the pet. Example: 1

args.name: petId
args.description: The numeric ID of the pet belonging to the owner. Example: 2

args.name: lastName
args.description: Optional last name filter for owners. Pass empty string to list all. Example: 'Smith'

args.name: ownerId
args.description: The numeric ID of the owner to retrieve. Example: 1

tests.minimum_expectation: at least one meaningful MCP test verifying tool response for listPetTypes or getPet, or explicit skip with documented reason

target_files.legacy-to-mcp13/input-documents/petclini: MicroProfile REST Client interface declaring all 7 GET endpoints with @RegisterRestClient(configKey='petclinic') and @Produces(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)
DTO for PetType schema: id (int), name (string maxLength 80)
DTO for Visit schema: id, date, description and related fields
DTO for Pet schema: id, name, birthDate, type (PetType), ownerId, visits (List<Visit>)
DTO for Owner schema: id, firstName, lastName, address, city, telephone, pets (List<Pet>)
Quarkus MCP server class with @Tool-annotated methods wrapping each REST client endpoint, returning ToolResponse with TextContent
Set port 8888, CORS enabled, and rest-client.petclinic.url
Add sidecar container for petclinic-mcp image exposing port 8888 to the existing Deployment
Quarkus test for MCP server tools, at minimum verifying listPetTypes tool invocation

mcpTools.name: listPetTypes
mcpTools.description: Lists all pet types available in the petclinic system. Takes no parameters. Returns a JSON array of PetType objects each with id and name fields.

mcpTools.name: getPetType
mcpTools.description: Retrieves a single pet type by its numeric ID. Parameter: petTypeId (int) — the ID of the pet type to look up, e.g. 1. Returns a single PetType object with id and name.

args.name: petTypeId
args.description: The numeric ID of the pet type to retrieve. Example: 1

mcpTools.name: listPets
mcpTools.description: Lists all pets registered in the petclinic system. Takes no parameters. Returns a JSON array of Pet objects including id, name, birthDate, type, ownerId, and visits.

mcpTools.name: getPet
mcpTools.description: Retrieves a single pet by its numeric ID. Parameter: petId (int) — the ID of the pet, e.g. 3. Returns a Pet object with id, name, birthDate, type, ownerId, and visits.

args.name: petId
args.description: The numeric ID of the pet to retrieve. Example: 3

mcpTools.name: getOwnersPet
mcpTools.description: Retrieves a specific pet belonging to a specific owner. Parameters: ownerId (int) — the owner's ID, e.g. 1; petId (int) — the pet's ID, e.g. 2. Returns a Pet object.

args.name: ownerId
args.description: The numeric ID of the owner who owns the pet. Example: 1

args.name: petId
args.description: The numeric ID of the pet belonging to the given owner. Example: 2

mcpTools.name: listOwners
mcpTools.description: Lists owners in the petclinic system, optionally filtered by last name. Parameter: lastName (string, optional) — partial or full last name filter, e.g. 'Smith'. Returns a JSON array of Owner objects.

args.name: lastName
args.description: Optional last name to filter owners. Pass empty string to return all owners. Example: 'Smith'

mcpTools.name: getOwner
mcpTools.description: Retrieves a single owner by their numeric ID. Parameter: ownerId (int) — the ID of the owner, e.g. 1. Returns an Owner object with id, firstName, lastName, address, city, telephone, and pets.

args.name: ownerId
args.description: The numeric ID of the owner to retrieve. Example: 1

kubernetesChanges.description: Add a sidecar container to the existing Deployment spec under spec.template.spec.containers

steps.task: Create Pet DTO referencing PetType and Visit
Create PetclinicService REST client interface with @RegisterRestClient(configKey='petclinic') and all 7 GET endpoints annotated with @Produces(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)

Create PetclinicMcpServer with @ApplicationScoped, inject PetclinicService with @Inject @RestClient, implement one @Tool method per endpoint with @ToolArg on each parameter, return ToolResponse containing TextContent with JSON-serialized result
Write application.properties with port 8888, CORS enabled, and rest-client.petclinic.url
Update Kubernetes manifest to add sidecar container petclinic-mcp exposing port 8888
Write Quarkus test for PetclinicMcpServer verifying at least one tool invocation

dependencies.notes: All required dependencies are already present in pom.xml. No changes needed.

tech_debt.configKey_vs_baseUri: Rule 02 specifies baseUri attribute on @RegisterRestClient but the plan enforces configKey per global constraints; configKey is the correct modern approach and avoids hardcoding URLs in source
tech_debt.Visit_schema_incomplete: Visit DTO fields beyond date and description were not fully specified in the prompt; Visit is defined with common fields but may need adjustment if the full OpenAPI schema differs
tech_debt.no_error_handling_in_tools: @Tool methods must not declare throws; runtime exceptions from the REST client will propagate unchecked — consider wrapping in try/catch and returning error TextContent

evaluation_criteria: @Inject and @RestClient both present on the PetclinicService field in PetclinicMcpServer
No throws declared on any @Tool method
No new ObjectMapper() instantiation — use injected ObjectMapper or Jakarta JSON-B
At least one meaningful MCP test or explicit skip reason documented in test file
Sidecar container in Kubernetes manifest exposes containerPort 8888
Each @Tool description mentions its parameters and return value
All four DTOs (PetType, Visit, Pet, Owner) created in org.acme.dto package
PetclinicService interface name ends with 'Service' per Rule 02
